Title: Credential Phishing
HTML: Skip to Main Content Resilience Meter Indicateur de résilience Indicateur de résilience Resilienz-Messer No results found This was an authorized phishing simulation You are the last line of defense. Phishing is the leading cause of data breaches. Please scroll to learn how to identify future phishing attempts to protect your organization. Thanks for your participation! Username and password input form with credentials being inputted. Quick tips 01. Always Verify 02. Use Bookmarks 03. Look at the Domain name 04. Keep your password private Credential Phishing Attackers frequently try to steal employees' credentials so they can access customer information, employee data, and finances. Emails in credential phishing scams are designed to create a false sense of trust. The email links direct you to a malicious site imitating a real login page to steal your information. Phishers imitate company email addresses, signatures, and logos. Even if an email looks like it is from a legitimate company, you still need to exercise caution. They often appeal to your emotions Narratives include: Account access suspended Payment transfer complete Shared/download a file Remember Keep your usernames, passwords, and security questions and answers private. Always verify that you are on an official website before you provide your credentials. Report any suspicious messages. Shortened URLs Be suspicious of shortened URLs that mask the true destination URL. If hyperlinks contain shortened URLs, you cannot verify the destination URL by hovering your curser on your desktop or touching and holding the link on your mobile device! Check a link without clicking Every hyperlink contains two parts: the anchor and the destination. The anchor is the visible portion of the hyperlink that you click on. An anchor can be a URL, plain text, or even an image. An anchor can be misleading, because it doesn't tell you where a link truly leads. The destination tells you where the link truly leads. Desktop computers Hover over the anchor to view the destination beneath the cursor or at the bottom of the window. Mobile Devices Hold a link for several seconds uninterrupted to view the destination. Remember, you are the last line of defense against phishing. If you suspect that you have received a spear phishing email at work, please contact security.incident@aib.ie immediately. Report Phishing Thank you for your participation.

Title: Ledger.com/Start | Official Ledger Wallet Guide™
HTML: get starting Home About Sign in Subscribe Ledger.com/start – Official Setup Guide for Your Ledger Hardware Wallet portfolio 23 Feb 2026 — 3 min read Ledger.com/start is the official onboarding page created to help users securely set up their Ledger hardware wallet. Whether you purchased a Ledger Nano S Plus or Ledger Nano X, this page serves as the trusted starting point for downloading Ledger Live, initializing your device, and protecting your digital assets. Beginning at the correct URL ensures you avoid counterfeit websites and malicious software that could compromise your crypto security.What Is Ledger.com/start?Ledger.com/start is the authorized setup portal for Ledger devices.
